definitions.text: Chiefly preceded by the: superlative form of little: most little.
Chiefly used with abstract nouns: less than all others in extent or size; littlest, smallest.
Of two or more animals or plants with the same name: the smaller or smallest.
Chiefly preceded by the: superlative form of little: most little. Smallest in amount or extent.
examples: He earns the least money in his family.
Of all the sisters, she has the least patience.
He earns the least money in his family.
Of all the sisters, she has the least patience.

definitions.text: In a degree below all others; in the lowest or smallest degree.
examples: My best routine was the one I had practised the least.
I don’t much like housework, and doing the dishes least of all.

definitions.text: Used to form superlatives of adjectives, especially those that do not form the superlative by adding -est.
examples: It was the least surprising thing.

definitions.text: Preceded by the: superlative form of little: most little; the lowest-ranking or most insignificant person or group of people.
examples: The pastor preached that even the least of God’s creatures deserves freedom from needless or idle cruelty.

definitions.text: Something of the smallest possible extent; an indivisible unit; a minimum.
Chiefly preceded by the: superlative form of little: most little; the smallest amount or extent of something.
examples: I’m giving least of all towards her present.
